About

A Career Defined by
Global Service

Curtis Raynold is an Independent Consultant in International Affairs at Curtis Raynold Consulting, LLC. At the time of his retirement in 2016, he was Secretary to the United Nations Secretary General’s Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters.

Joining the UN in 1985 through the UN National Competitive Recruitment Exam in Economics, he served with the UN Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) in Vienna, Austria from 1985 to 2004. Following the negotiated cease-fire between NATO and Yugoslavia and the adoption of UN Security Council Resolution 1244, he was posted to the UN Interim Administration Mission in Kosovo (UNMIK) in October 1999.

Prior to his international engagement, Mr. Raynold was a diplomat in the St. Lucia Foreign Service from 1981 to 1985.

“Bridging diplomacy, economics, and international law across four decades of global service.”

Career

Professional Timeline

1981 – 1985
Diplomat
St. Lucia Foreign Service
Represented St. Lucia’s diplomatic interests abroad, building foundational expertise in international relations and foreign policy.
1985 – 2004
Officer, UNODC
UN Office on Drugs & Crime · Vienna, Austria
Entered the UN through the National Competitive Recruitment Exam in Economics. Served nearly two decades addressing international drug control and crime prevention.
October 1999
Posted to UNMIK
UN Interim Administration Mission in Kosovo
Following the NATO–Yugoslavia cease-fire and UN Security Council Resolution 1244, deployed to Kosovo as part of the UN’s interim civilian administration.
2004 – 2016
Secretary, Advisory Board
UN SG’s Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters
Served as Secretary to the advisory body providing the UN Secretary General expert guidance on disarmament and international peace and security.
2016 – Present
Independent Consultant
Curtis Raynold Consulting, LLC
Following retirement from the UN, established a consulting practice providing expert counsel on international affairs, diplomacy, and global governance.

Services

Areas of Expertise

01
International Affairs Consulting
Strategic counsel on global issues informed by decades of on-the-ground UN experience across Europe, the Caribbean, and conflict-affected regions.
02
Disarmament & Security Policy
Expert analysis and advisory on arms control, disarmament frameworks, and international peace and security mechanisms.
03
Diplomatic Advisory
Guidance for governments, NGOs, and institutions navigating complex multilateral environments and UN engagement strategies.
04
Post-Conflict Governance
Drawing on firsthand UNMIK experience in Kosovo, providing insight into post-conflict reconstruction, institution-building, and rule of law.
